name: Acme SaaS Buyer Standard
description: Acme's negotiation positions for inbound SaaS subscription agreements — the standard our procurement team enforces on every vendor paper.

custom_rules.title: Liability cap must be at least 12x fees
custom_rules.description: We do not accept a liability cap below 12x trailing fees.

custom_rules.title: Termination notice must be 30 days or fewer
custom_rules.description: We do not accept a termination-for-convenience notice longer than 30 days.

custom_rules.title: Governing law must be Delaware or New York
custom_rules.description: We accept only Delaware or New York as the governing law.

custom_rules.title: No mandatory arbitration
custom_rules.description: We strike mandatory-arbitration clauses; disputes go to court.

custom_rules.title: A Data Processing Addendum must be referenced
custom_rules.description: The agreement must define and incorporate a Data Processing Addendum.

guidance.ideal: 12x trailing fees — our opening and preferred position.
guidance.acceptable: 6x fees is our floor; do not settle lower without sign-off.
guidance.walk_away: Below 6x — escalate to the deal lead before agreeing.
Longer than 60 days locks us in — push back.

guidance.acceptable: New York or California are acceptable.
guidance.walk_away: Any other state — route to legal before agreeing.

guidance.ideal: 30 days to cure a breach before the vendor can terminate.
guidance.acceptable: 15 days is the shortest cure window we accept.
guidance.walk_away: Under 15 days leaves us no room to remedy — push back.

guidance.ideal: 99.95% monthly uptime — our target for production workloads.
guidance.acceptable: 99.9% is the floor for a business-critical service.
guidance.walk_away: Below 99.9% — escalate or require service credits.

guidance.ideal: Mutual indemnity — both sides carry the obligation. Hold.
guidance.acceptable: One-way indemnity is present — push for a mutual carve-out.
guidance.walk_away: No indemnity at all — escalate to the deal lead.
